reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: The patch looks good and addresses the issue of binary size increase. However, it would be beneficial to add a comment explaining why this change was necessary.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: The patch is a good step towards reducing binary size. However, we should also consider the performance impact of this change.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: The patch extracts the percpu_up_read() slowpath into a new __percpu_up_read() function to avoid binary size increase from adding a tracepoint to an inlined function.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the patch without raising any technical concerns or suggestions.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, 10 Mar 2026 17:49:38 +0000 Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

> Move the percpu_up_read() slowpath out of the inline function into a new
> __percpu_up_read() to avoid binary size increase from adding a
> tracepoint to an inlined function.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
> ---
>  include/linux/percpu-rwsem.h  | 15 +++------------
>  kernel/locking/percpu-rwsem.c | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
>  2 files changed, 21 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/include/linux/percpu-rwsem.h b/include/linux/percpu-rwsem.h
> index c8cb010d655e..39d5bf8e6562 100644
> --- a/include/linux/percpu-rwsem.h
> +++ b/include/linux/percpu-rwsem.h
> @@ -107,6 +107,8 @@ static inline bool percpu_down_read_trylock(struct percpu_rw_semaphore *sem)
>  	return ret;
>  }
>  

Acked-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch series extracts the percpu_up_read() slowpath into a new __percpu_up_read() function to avoid binary size increase due to adding a tracepoint to an inlined function.

2026-03-16.patch_summary: The patch extracts the percpu_up_read() slowpath into a new __percpu_up_read() function to avoid binary size increase from adding a tracepoint to an inlined function.
